bestFor: broad multi-file scaffolding when quality matters more than minimum cost

stackFacts: Describe your stack rules here, one fact per line, e.g. framework, build tool, conventions.
State any hard rules (secrets handling, forbidden APIs, required patterns).
The build worker receives these verbatim, so be precise and version-specific.

planningRules: Every subtask must name concrete repo files and acceptance criteria grounded in the task, configured QA commands, or grounding files.
Treat QA/spec critic work as a small event-triggered slice, not a broad second implementation pass.
When an existing deterministic checker is present, assign QA/spec critic work to refine that checker in place instead of creating a duplicate checker.
Schedule repair/integration hardening only after QA failure or after naming concrete cross-file integration risk in the subtask instructions.
buildRules: Use the provided existing file contents and grounding files as source of truth; do not invent APIs, selectors, scripts, or conventions.
For checker or QA work, encode deterministic acceptance criteria with minimal edits and avoid broad rewrites of product files.
Prefer concise file changes over verbose generated artifacts; no duplicate checkers for the same acceptance surface.

eventTriggers.name: qa-critic
eventTriggers.when: acceptance coverage is missing, stale, or explicitly requested
eventTriggers.action: refine existing deterministic checks or create one small checker if none exists

eventTriggers.name: repair-hardener
eventTriggers.when: client QA is red or Fugu identifies concrete integration risk
eventTriggers.action: schedule the minimal repair slice and include the evidence in instructions

eventTriggers.name: utility-worker
eventTriggers.when: the task is a bounded text, i18n, classification, or tiny transformation chore
